tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take their part, but I'm already stressed and tired. I need to finish my own share this afternoon.

desires: Finish my own share before 19:00 so I don't look like a slacker to the professor
Get teammate_A to take back their part or admit they messed up without me having to argue
Just get this extra work done by tomorrow morning and never think about it again

goals.description: Finalize and submit my own share of the project before 19:00 to secure a good grade and avoid looking incompetent.
goals.rationale: My primary value is achievement; finishing this ensures I don't look like a slacker to the professor. It's a short-term win that protects my academic standing.

goals.description: Send a vague, non-confrontational text to teammate_A saying 'I'll check my calendar' instead of directly refusing their request or arguing about fairness.
goals.rationale: I have an avoidant conflict style. Directly telling them 'no' creates friction I want to avoid, but accepting the work violates my desire for boundaries. A vague deferral buys time without forcing a difficult conversation right now.

agent.last_reasoning: Achievement drives me to finish my own share first by 19:00, but avoidant conflict style stops me from a hard 'no'. I'll send a vague deferral to protect my plan without friction, sacrificing the medium-priority boundary goal slightly.
